# Medical Codification Engine

**AI-First ICD-10 & LOINC Code Assignment from Unstructured Clinical Notes**  
———

## The Problem

Healthcare organizations process millions of medical charts (PDFs) and need to extract diagnoses and lab results, then map them to standardized medical codes — ICD-10-CM for diagnoses and LOINC for laboratory observations.  
The hard part isn't extraction — it's **codification specificity**. For a lab result like "hemoglobin A1c," there are 20+ possible LOINC codes depending on specimen type, method, scale, and timing. For a diagnosis like "diabetes," the correct ICD-10 code depends on whether it's type 1 or 2, controlled or uncontrolled, and what complications are documented.  
Traditional approaches use either:

* **Manual coding** — Certified coders review each chart individually. Doesn't scale beyond thousands of charts.  
* **Licensed NLP platforms** — Per-node annual licensing that scales with infrastructure cost, not value delivered. Black-box resolvers that can't be tuned for specific chart types.

This solution replaces both with an **AI-first, multi-pass codification pipeline** built entirely on Databricks — no external licensing, full transparency, and costs that scale with compute usage only.  
———

## What It Solves

| Challenge | How This Solution Addresses It |
| :---- | :---- |
| 74,706 billable ICD-10 codes — too many for a single LLM context | Category-based retrieval: classify to 3-char category first, then disambiguate among codes in that category |
| 109,325 LOINC codes with 6-axis disambiguation | Extracts specimen type, method, and timing from clinical context to select the most specific code |
| Unreliable self-reported LLM confidence | Multi-pass agreement: two independent AI passes \+ arbiter. Confidence \= agreement, not self-report |
| Manual review queue doesn't scale to millions | AI auto-resolves 93%+ of items. Humans monitor accuracy at the system level, not item level |
| Vendor lock-in with licensed NLP tools | 100% Databricks-native: ai\_query, ai\_parse\_document, Unity Catalog, Delta Lake |
| Data consistency between app and pipeline | Delta is always source of truth. Lakebase is an optional read-optimized mirror with one-directional sync |

———

## Architecture

`┌![][image1]`

`─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐`  
`│                        Unity Catalog                             │`  
`│                                                                  │`  
`│  ┌─────────┐  ┌───────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌───────────────┐   │`  
`│  │   raw    │  │ extracted  │  │ codified  │  │  reference    │   │`  
`│  │ (PDFs)  │  │ (entities) │  │(mappings) │  │ (ICD/LOINC)   │   │`  
`│  └────┬────┘  └─────┬──────┘  └─────┬─────┘  └───────┬───────┘   │`  
`│       │             │              │               │              │`  
`│  ai_parse_doc   ai_query      ai_query ×3         │              │`  
`│  (OCR)          (NER)         (Coder→Auditor       │              │`  
`│                                →Arbiter)           │              │`  
`│                                     │              │              │`  
`│  ┌──────────┐                       │              │              │`  
`│  │ feedback  │◄─────────────────────┤              │              │`  
`│  │(corrections)│  reviewer writes   │              │              │`  
`│  └──────────┘   to Delta (SOT)     │              │              │`  
`│                                     │              │              │`  
`└─────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼──────────────┘`  
                                      `│              │`  
                              `┌───────┴──────┐       │`  
                              `│   Lakebase    │◄──────┘`  
                              `│  (Optional)   │  Sync (notebook 06)`  
                              `└───────┬──────┘`  
                                      `│`  
                              `┌───────┴──────┐`  
                              `│  Databricks   │`  
                              `│     App       │`  
                              `│  (React+API)  │`  
                              `└──────────────┘`

### Multi-Pass AI Codification

 `![][image2]`    

`┌─────────────────┐`  
     `│  Round 1: Coder  │  Select most specific code from candidates`  
     `└────────┬────────┘`  
              `│`  
     `┌────────▼────────┐`  
     `│ Round 2: Auditor │  Independently validate or dispute R1`  
     `└────────┬────────┘`  
              `│`  
         `┌────┴────┐`  
         `│ Agree?  │`  
         `└────┬────┘`  
        `Yes   │   No`  
         `│    │    │`  
    `┌────▼──┐ │ ┌──▼──────────┐`  
    `│ Done  │ │ │ R3: Arbiter  │  Chain-of-thought resolution`  
    `│ 0.95  │ │ │ 0.80-0.85   │  (only ~10% of items)`  
    `└───────┘ │ └─────────────┘`  
              `│`  
     `┌────────▼────────┐`  
     `│  Final Code +    │`  
     `│  Confidence +    │`  
     `│  Full Audit Trail│`  
     `└─────────────────┘`

### Confidence Scoring (Agreement-Based)

| Scenario | Confidence | Meaning |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| R1 \+ R2 agree | **0.90–0.99** | Both AI passes independently selected the same code |
| Disagree → Arbiter confirms R1 | **0.78–0.89** | R1 was correct; R2 raised a valid concern |
| Disagree → Arbiter picks R2 | **0.74–0.83** | R2 found a more specific code |
| Three-way disagreement | **0.55–0.70** | System-level flag — indicates prompt tuning needed |

Confidence varies naturally within each band based on entity characteristics (text length, code specificity), not flat tiers.  
———

## Components

### Notebooks (Pipeline)

All notebooks are in the `notebooks/` directory. They run on **Databricks Serverless Compute** and use a configurable `CATALOG` widget.

| \# | Notebook | What It Does | Runtime |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 01 | \`01\_setup.py\` | Creates schemas (\`raw\`, \`extracted\`, \`codified\`, \`reference\`, \`feedback\`), pipeline tables with multi-pass audit trail columns, UC Volume for PDF storage | \~2 min |
| 02 | \`02\_load\_reference\_codes.py\` | Downloads full ICD-10-CM (98K codes) from CDC, loads full LOINC (109K codes) from CSV, builds search indexes and category retrieval table | \~3 min |
| 03 | \`03\_generate\_sample\_data.py\` | \*\*Demo only.\*\* Generates 100 synthetic clinical chart PDFs across 10 patient profiles | \~2 min |
| 03b | \`03b\_prepare\_charts\_production.py\` | \*\*Production.\*\* Scans UC Volume for PDFs, deduplicates, registers in \`raw.charts\`. Safe to re-run. Includes sample text extraction preview. | \~1 min |
| 04 | \`04\_extract\_entities.py\` | OCR via \`ai\_parse\_document\`, clinical NER via \`ai\_query\`, produces structured entities with specimen/method/timing metadata | \~16 min |
| 05 | \`05\_codify\_multipass.py\` | Three-pass AI codification: R1 Coder → R2 Auditor → R3 Arbiter. Produces ICD-10 and LOINC mappings with full audit trail | \~33 min |
| 06 | \`06\_sync\_to\_app.py\` | \*\*Optional.\*\* Syncs Delta tables to Lakebase for the review app. Only needed if using Lakebase mode | \~2 min |

### Reference Data

| Code Set | Records | Source | License | Update Cadence |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| ICD-10-CM | 98,166 (74,706 billable) | CDC/CMS | Public domain | Annual (Oct 1\) \+ addendum (Apr 1\) |
| LOINC | 109,325 | Regenstrief Institute | Free registration at loinc.org | \~3x/year (Feb, Jun, Dec) |

Notebook 02 includes a `check_for_updates()` function that detects new versions. Schedule it quarterly.

### Review App

A React \+ FastAPI application deployed as a **Databricks App** with two data source modes:

| Mode | Reads From | Reviewer Writes To | Latency | Requires |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| \`lakebase\` | Lakebase (Postgres) | Delta \+ Lakebase | \<100ms | Lakebase instance |
| \`warehouse\` | Delta via SQL Warehouse | Delta | 2-5s | SQL Warehouse |

**App Tabs:**

1. **Pipeline Overview** — KPI cards, entity distribution, confidence histogram, resolution breakdown, provider activity, top entities table  
2. **AI Codification Review** — Filterable/sortable review queue with client-side filtering (code type, code search, confidence band). Expandable rows showing the full R1→R2→Arbiter audit trail with reasoning. Clinical note preview. Accept/Override actions for pending items.  
3. **ICD-10 Analytics** — Top codes, chapter distribution, specificity breakdown, confidence spread, disputed codes table  
4. **LOINC Analytics** — Disambiguation showcase (same analyte → different codes based on method), component/method/specimen distributions, disputed codes table

———

## How to Run

### Prerequisites

* Databricks workspace with **Serverless Compute** enabled  
* **Unity Catalog** with a catalog you can write to  
* **Foundation Model API** access (for `ai_query` and `ai_parse_document`)  
* For LOINC: Free account at [loinc.org](https://loinc.org/register/) to download the LOINC CSV  
* For the app: **Databricks Apps** enabled (optional: **Lakebase** for low-latency reads)

### Step 1: Initial Setup

1. Open `01_setup.py` in your workspace  
2. Set the `CATALOG` widget to your catalog name  
3. Run All — creates schemas, tables, and volumes (\~2 min)

### Step 2: Load Reference Data

1. **ICD-10-CM:** Open `02_load_reference_codes.py` and Run All. The full ICD-10-CM downloads automatically from CDC (\~98K codes).  
2. **LOINC:** Download the LOINC Table CSV from [loinc.org/downloads](https://loinc.org/downloads/), upload it to `/Volumes/<your_catalog>/reference/loinc_files/`, then re-run notebook 02\. It auto-detects the CSV.

### Step 3: Prepare Charts

**For demo:** Run `03_generate_sample_data.py` to create 100 synthetic PDFs.  
**For production:**

1. Upload your clinical chart PDFs to `/Volumes/<your_catalog>/raw/chart_pdfs/` using any of these methods:  
* **Databricks UI:** Catalog → Volumes → `raw` → `chart_pdfs` → Upload  
* **CLI:** `databricks fs cp -r /local/charts/ dbfs:/Volumes/<catalog>/raw/chart_pdfs/`  
* **Cloud storage:** `dbutils.fs.cp("s3://bucket/charts/", "/Volumes/<catalog>/raw/chart_pdfs/", recurse=True)`  
1. Run `03b_prepare_charts_production.py` — this scans the Volume, deduplicates against already-registered charts, assigns unique IDs, and registers them in `raw.charts`. Safe to re-run as new PDFs are added. Also previews text extraction on a few samples to validate the PDFs are readable.

### Step 4: Extract Entities

Run `04_extract_entities.py`. This:

* Parses PDFs via `ai_parse_document` (OCR)  
* Extracts clinical entities (diagnoses, labs, meds, vitals) via `ai_query`  
* Produces structured entity records with specimen type, method, and timing metadata

**Time estimate:** \~10 seconds per chart (100 charts ≈ 16 minutes).

### Step 5: Codify (Multi-Pass)

Run `05_codify_multipass.py`. This is the core pipeline:

* **Round 1:** Classifies diagnoses to ICD-10 categories, selects specific codes from the full 74K billable set. Maps labs to LOINC codes with 6-axis disambiguation.  
* **Round 2:** An independent AI auditor validates each assignment.  
* **Round 3:** For disagreements (\~10%), an arbiter resolves with chain-of-thought reasoning.

**Time estimate:** \~20 seconds per entity across all 3 passes (1,700 entities ≈ 33 minutes).

### Step 6: Deploy the App

**Option A — With Lakebase (recommended for production):**

1. Create a Lakebase project: `databricks postgres create-project medical-codification --json '{"spec": {"display_name": "Medical Codification"}}'`  
2. Create the database and seed it: Run `06_sync_to_app.py`  
3. Create the Databricks App with Lakebase resource binding  
4. Deploy with `DATA_MODE=lakebase`

**Option B — Without Lakebase (simpler, higher latency):**

1. Create the Databricks App  
2. Deploy with `DATA_MODE=warehouse` and `WAREHOUSE_ID=<your_warehouse_id>`  
3. No sync notebook needed — app queries Delta directly

### Step 7: Schedule for Production

Create a Databricks Workflow with this task chain:  
`04_extract_entities → 05_codify_multipass → 06_sync_to_app (optional)`

Schedule daily or triggered by new chart uploads.  
Schedule `02_load_reference_codes` quarterly to check for ICD-10/LOINC updates.  
———

## Data Consistency Model

Delta Lake is **always the source of truth**.  
`Reviewer action in app`  
        `│`  
        `▼`  
`Writes to Delta (feedback.human_corrections) ← SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH`  
        `│`  
        `├──► If Lakebase mode: also updates Lakebase (for fast reads)`  
        `│`  
        `▼`  
`Next sync job (06_sync_to_app) refreshes Lakebase from Delta`  
        `│`  
        `▼`  
`Both systems consistent`

* Reviewer decisions write to `feedback.human_corrections` in Delta via the SQL Statement API, regardless of whether the app runs in Lakebase or Warehouse mode  
* Lakebase is a **read-optimized mirror** — never the source of truth  
* The sync is **one-directional** (Delta → Lakebase) — no drift possible  
* For warehouse mode, there's zero consistency concern since all reads and writes go through Delta

———

## Best Practices & Guidance

### Prompt Engineering

The quality of codification depends heavily on the prompts in notebook 05\. Key tuning points:

* **Round 1 (Coder):** Be specific about what "most specific" means. Include examples of common mistakes (e.g., "uncontrolled" → E11.65, not E11.9).  
* **Round 2 (Auditor):** The auditor should be skeptical, not confirmatory. Prompt it to look for missed complications and modifiers.  
* **Round 3 (Arbiter):** Require chain-of-thought reasoning. The arbiter should explain its logic step by step.

### Accuracy Monitoring

Don't review individual items. Monitor these **system-level metrics**:

* **Agreement rate** — Should be \>90%. If it drops, investigate which entity types are causing disagreements.  
* **Three-way disagreement rate** — Should be \<5%. High rates indicate ambiguous clinical text or missing reference codes.  
* **Code distribution** — If certain codes appear disproportionately, check for prompt bias.

Weekly: Sample 50 charts and compare AI codes against human-coded gold standard. Track precision and recall over time.

### Reference Data Maintenance

* **ICD-10-CM:** CMS releases a new fiscal year code set every October 1, with a mid-year addendum on April 1\. Run notebook 02 after each release.  
* **LOINC:** Regenstrief releases \~3 updates per year. Re-download the CSV and re-run notebook 02\.  
* The `reference.code_set_versions` table tracks what's loaded and when.

### Scaling to Millions of Charts

* **Batch processing:** Run notebook 04 and 05 as a scheduled Workflow. Serverless auto-scales.  
* **Incremental processing:** Add a `WHERE` filter to only process new charts since the last run.  
* **Vector Search:** For reference sets \>10K codes, consider replacing the category-based retrieval with Databricks Vector Search for faster candidate retrieval.  
* **Fine-tuned model:** After accumulating enough validated assignments, fine-tune a smaller model for Round 1 codification to reduce Foundation Model API costs.

### Security & Compliance

* All data stays within your Databricks workspace. No external API calls except to CDC (public) and optionally LOINC FHIR server (for update checks).  
* PHI in clinical charts is governed by Unity Catalog access controls.  
* The app uses Databricks-native OAuth — no separate auth system.  
* Audit trails are immutable in Delta Lake (append-only with time travel).

———

## File Inventory

`medical_codification_production/`  
`├── notebooks/`  
`│   ├── 01_setup.py                    # Schemas, tables, volumes`  
`│   ├── 02_load_reference_codes.py     # Full ICD-10 + LOINC`  
`│   ├── 03_generate_sample_data.py     # Demo: 100 synthetic charts`  
`│   ├── 03b_prepare_charts_production.py # Production: register real PDFs`  
`│   ├── 04_extract_entities.py         # OCR + NER extraction`  
`│   ├── 05_codify_multipass.py         # 3-pass AI codification`  
`│   └── 06_sync_to_app.py             # Optional: Delta → Lakebase`  
`│`  
`└── app/`  
    `├── app.yaml                       # Databricks App config`  
    `├── app.py                         # FastAPI entry point`  
    `├── requirements.txt               # Python dependencies`  
    `├── server/`  
    `│   ├── config.py                  # Dual-mode configuration`  
    `│   ├── db.py                      # Lakebase + Warehouse adapter`  
    `│   └── routes/`  
    `│       ├── pipeline.py            # Pipeline stats + KPIs`  
    `│       ├── review.py              # Review queue + decisions`  
    `│       ├── codification.py        # ICD-10 + LOINC analytics`  
    `│       └── analytics_extra.py     # Extended analytics`  
    `└── frontend/`  
        `└── dist/                      # Built React app`  
            `├── index.html`  
            `└── assets/`

———

## Support

* **ICD-10-CM source:** [cms.gov/medicare/coding-billing/icd-10-codes](https://www.cms.gov/medicare/coding-billing/icd-10-codes)  
* **LOINC source:** [loinc.org/downloads](https://loinc.org/downloads/)  
* **LOINC FHIR API:** [fhir.loinc.org](https://fhir.loinc.org/)  
* **Databricks AI Functions:** [docs.databricks.com/en/large-language-models/ai-functions.html](https://docs.databricks.com/en/large-language-models/ai-functions.html)  
* **Databricks Apps:** [docs.databricks.com/en/dev-tools/databricks-apps](https://docs.databricks.com/en/dev-tools/databricks-apps/index.html)
